project_history.name: Daily Digest: Personalized News Aggregator (Python & Mobile Web)
project_history.description: Build a Python-based news aggregator that fetches articles from various sources, categorizes them, and presents them through a simple, mobile-friendly web interface. You'll learn web scraping, data handling, API development with Flask, and basic front-end design for mobile-first experiences.

daily_tasks.title: Python Setup & Basics Refresher
daily_tasks.description: Install Python, pip, and a code editor (e.g., VS Code). Review fundamental Python concepts like variables, data types, loops, and functions. Set up a virtual environment for the project to manage dependencies.

daily_tasks.title: Introduction to Web Scraping
daily_tasks.description: Understand the basics of HTTP requests and responses. Install the 'requests' and 'BeautifulSoup4' libraries. Write a simple Python script to fetch the content of a webpage and parse its HTML to extract the page title.

daily_tasks.title: Scraping a Single News Source
daily_tasks.description: Identify a simple news website or blog section to scrape. Inspect its HTML structure using browser developer tools to locate news article titles and their corresponding links. Develop a Python script to extract these specific elements.

daily_tasks.title: Data Handling & Storage
daily_tasks.description: Refine your scraping script to extract more details from each news item, such as a brief summary or an image URL (if easily available). Store the collected news data as a list of dictionaries in Python, then save this structured data into a JSON file.

daily_tasks.title: Introduction to Flask Framework
daily_tasks.description: Learn about the concept of web frameworks and specifically Flask's core components like routes and view functions. Install Flask and create your first 'Hello, World!' Flask application. Run the application locally to understand its basic operation.

daily_tasks.title: Building a Simple API Endpoint
daily_tasks.description: Load the news data from your previously saved JSON file into your Flask application. Create a new Flask route (e.g., '/news') that serves all the news items as a JSON response. Test this API endpoint using your web browser or a tool like Postman/Insomnia.

daily_tasks.title: Enhancing Scraping for Multiple Sources
daily_tasks.description: Identify a second news source to include in your aggregator. Adapt your existing scraping logic to handle the HTML structure of this new source. Modify your data storage mechanism to combine and manage news items from both sources effectively.

daily_tasks.title: Implementing Basic Categorization
daily_tasks.description: Define a few simple news categories (e.g., 'Technology', 'Sports', 'General'). Implement a basic Python function that assigns a category to each news item based on keywords found in its title or description. Update your news data with these assigned categories.

daily_tasks.title: Refining API with Filtering
daily_tasks.description: Enhance your '/news' API endpoint by adding a query parameter (e.g., '/news?category=Technology'). Modify the API's logic to filter the news items based on the provided category parameter. Thoroughly test the filtered API endpoints to ensure correct functionality.

daily_tasks.title: Introduction to HTML & CSS for Mobile
daily_tasks.description: Learn fundamental HTML tags for structuring web content (div, p, h1, a, img). Explore basic CSS properties for styling (colors, fonts, margins, padding). Create a simple HTML page with a mobile-friendly viewport meta tag and apply basic responsive styling.

daily_tasks.title: Designing a Mobile-First News Layout
daily_tasks.description: Design the visual layout for a single news card, including elements like the title, image, summary, and a link to the full article. Create a main container for displaying multiple news cards and apply basic CSS Flexbox or Grid properties to ensure mobile responsiveness.

daily_tasks.title: Integrating Frontend with Flask API
daily_tasks.description: Learn about Flask's built-in templating engine, Jinja2. Create a Flask route (e.g., '/') that renders an HTML template. Pass the news data from your Flask backend to the Jinja2 template and dynamically display it within your mobile-friendly HTML structure.

daily_tasks.title: Adding Basic Interactivity
daily_tasks.description: Add simple navigation or filter buttons (e.g., 'All News', 'Technology') to your HTML template. Implement basic client-side filtering by linking these buttons to different Flask routes that serve category-specific news, providing a basic interactive experience.

daily_tasks.title: Project Review & Future Steps
daily_tasks.description: Conduct a comprehensive review of the entire project, ensuring all components (scraping, API, frontend) work seamlessly together. Identify potential improvements such as more robust scraping, advanced categorization, or user accounts. Research basic deployment options (e.g., Heroku, Render) to understand how to make your project live.
